title: China-to-Philippines EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China GB/T 18487 Baseline Versus Philippines Evidence Package
rows.target_requirement: For Philippines deployment, treat GB/T 18487 as a China-origin design baseline rather than destination compliance. The Philippines evidence package should map GB/T 18487 safety and control functions to the destination standard route, connector route, 60 Hz operating limits, EVIDA station obligations, utility interconnection file, and any BPS / PNS or project-specified IEC clauses.
target_standards: Destination PNS / IEC route where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly relies on GB/T 18487 for electric vehicle conductive charging system requirements, GB/T 20234 for connection devices, and GB/T 27930 for off-board DC charger to battery-management-system communication. These are not identical to the Philippines destination route and should be cross-referenced clause by clause.
rows.gap: The export gap is documentation and design conversion: nameplate ratings, 60 Hz validation, connector hardware, DC communication, EMC, local-language manuals, installation drawings, protective-device coordination, and site commissioning records must be converted into a Philippines-facing package.

mandatory_status.target: Destination package requirements are mandatory only where imposed by EVIDA, BPS / PNS, utility, ERC, electrical-installation, procurement, or local permitting routes
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 / GB/T 20234 / GB/T 27930 are China baseline standards and do not by themselves establish Philippines compliance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 18487 is a useful starting point, not a Philippines approval. Build a destination matrix that converts China safety, connector, EMC, grid, and installation evidence into the Philippines project route.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation / Standardization Administration of China

rows.target_requirement: IEC 62196 is the international plug, socket-outlet, vehicle connector, and vehicle inlet series used with conductive EV charging systems. Philippines-bound chargers should confirm the connector configuration required by the project, vehicle fleet, utility, or authority before shipment; IEC 62196 Type 1, Type 2, CCS, CHAdeMO, and other configurations are not physically interchangeable with China-only GB/T 20234 hardware.
target_standards: Project-specific Philippines EV fleet connector specification
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 20234 for AC and DC charging couplers, with China-specific geometry, pin allocation, control pilot, and DC communication assumptions. A GB/T 20234 connector set should be treated as a redesign item for Philippines deployments that specify IEC 62196-based connectors.
rows.gap: The connector gap is physical and protocol-level. Exporters should redesign couplers, sockets, locking, proximity / control-pilot circuits, DC charging communication, labelling, and spare-part strategy around the Philippines project connector route rather than assuming an adapter can close the compliance gap.

mandatory_status.target: Market and project interoperability requirement; mandatory where specified by procurement, permit condition, utility interconnection, or authority, but not stated here as a blanket whole-unit certification rule
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 20234 is the China domestic connector baseline and is not a substitute for IEC 62196 connector compatibility

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A GB/T 20234-only connector design should not be treated as Philippines-ready where the destination project expects IEC 62196-family interoperability. Confirm the exact connector route before certification testing and production tooling.

rows.requirement: EMC Evidence for Off-Board EV Charging Systems
rows.target_requirement: IEC 61851-21-2:2018 addresses EMC requirements for off-board electric vehicle charging systems. Philippines projects that cite IEC 61851 or PNS IEC adoptions should identify the applicable EMC clauses, test configuration, grid frequency, cable / connector set, communications module, and installation environment before relying on a China domestic EMC report.
target_standards: IEC 61000 series as referenced by the applicable EMC test plan
PNS IEC adoption where confirmed by BPS or project documents
rows.china_equivalent: China EMC files for EV chargers commonly reference GB/T 18487 test items and generic EMC standards such as GB/T 17799 series or GB 4824 where applicable. Those reports may not cover the IEC 61851-21-2:2018 off-board charger configuration, 60 Hz operation, Philippines connector set, or radio-module configuration.
rows.gap: Plan EMC retesting or a documented gap assessment when the exported configuration changes from the China file. Frequency, cable length, connector type, enclosure earthing, communication modules, and charging mode can change emissions and immunity results.

mandatory_status.target: Conditional; mandatory where required by a BPS / PNS route, project specification, utility acceptance, radio approval, or electrical-installation approval
mandatory_status.cn: EMC evidence is part of China technical conformity files where required, but China reports do not automatically satisfy Philippines IEC / PNS evidence needs

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not assume a 50 Hz China EMC report covers a Philippines IEC 61851-21-2:2018 charger configuration. Re-test or document the gap where frequency, connector, cable, enclosure, or communication modules differ.

rows.target_requirement: Republic Act No. 11697, the Electric Vehicle Industry Development Act, establishes the Philippines EV policy framework and includes charging-station and charging-infrastructure obligations for covered participants. For an imported charger, the product file should be separated from the site file: equipment safety evidence supports the installation, while DOE / local permitting, operation, tariffs, service rules, and designated charging-station obligations belong to the operator and project approval path.
target_standards: Republic Act No. 11697 - Electric Vehicle Industry Development Act
DOE EVIDA implementing rules and charging-station guidance where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China charger documentation commonly focuses on GB/T 18487 technical safety, GB/T 20234 connection devices, GB/T 27930 DC communication, factory quality records, and domestic installation practice. Those documents do not replace Philippines EVIDA charging-station obligations or DOE / local project requirements.
rows.gap: A charger test report is not a DOE charging-station approval. The Philippines project package should include operator responsibilities, site layout, electrical single-line drawings, metering and tariff assumptions, emergency shutdown and maintenance procedures, accessibility / user information where required, and evidence requested by the local government unit, utility, DOE, or ERC route.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for covered EVIDA charging-station participants and projects; not a blanket statement that every imported charger model has whole-unit BPS certification
mandatory_status.cn: China product standards and installation approvals apply domestically but do not replace Philippines EVIDA project obligations

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EVIDA is a Philippines project and operating-framework gap. Separate the product compliance file from the charging-station permit, operator, utility, and service-rule file.

source.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of the Philippines

rows.target_requirement: Philippines EV charging equipment must be engineered and commissioned for the local AC supply and grid-connection route. Exporters should confirm the applicable utility service voltage, phase arrangement, protection settings, metering, power-quality limits, and 60 Hz operation under the Philippine Grid Code / Distribution Code and the connecting distribution utility's requirements.
target_standards: Distribution utility interconnection and service requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic charger designs are commonly validated for 50 Hz AC input and China utility assumptions. GB/T 18487 evidence should be reviewed for input-frequency range, thermal performance, leakage-current behaviour, harmonic current, protection coordination, and metering accuracy at 60 Hz.
rows.gap: If a Chinese charger nameplate, firmware, protection relay, fan / pump motor, transformer, or EMC filter is specified only for 50 Hz, it should not be shipped as-is for Philippines use. Validate 60 Hz input operation, grid-code trip settings, harmonic performance, and utility connection documentation before installation.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection and installation acceptance where utility, electrical-code, ERC, Grid Code, or Distribution Code requirements apply
mandatory_status.cn: China 50 Hz validation is common for domestic installations but is not sufficient evidence for Philippines 60 Hz connection

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat 60 Hz as an engineering and utility-approval checkpoint. Re-check electrical ratings, firmware settings, harmonics, and protection coordination before Philippines commissioning.

rows.requirement: BPS / PNS Standards Route and IEC 61851 Technical Baseline
rows.target_requirement: The Bureau of Philippine Standards is the Philippines national standards body for Philippine National Standards. For EV chargers, exporters should confirm whether the current BPS catalogue or a buyer / authority specification cites PNS adoptions of the IEC 61851 conductive charging system series. Use bare IEC / PNS IEC standard numbers where the exact Philippine adoption edition cannot be confirmed from an official public listing.
target_standards: PNS IEC 61851 series where adopted by BPS
BPS technical regulations and current PNS catalogue scope
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 18487 for conductive charging system requirements and GB/T 20234 for connection devices. These GB standards are useful engineering references but should not be presented as Philippine PNS / IEC evidence unless a Philippine authority, customer specification, or conformity assessment body accepts the mapping.
rows.gap: Do not convert a China GB/T 18487 report into a Philippine claim by relabelling. Prepare a standards matrix that maps each IEC 61851 clause requested by the Philippines project to the Chinese design evidence, then identify any missing PNS / IEC edition, national deviation, language, or laboratory-acceptance item.

mandatory_status.target: Not confirmed as blanket whole-unit mandatory from this row; mandatory only where a BPS technical regulation, procurement document, permit condition, or authority requires the relevant PNS / IEC standard
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 / GB/T 20234 are the China domestic technical baseline for conductive charging interoperability

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat PNS / IEC 61851 evidence as a project and authority-scope requirement unless a current BPS instrument makes the exact charger category compulsory. Use bare standard numbers when the Philippine adoption edition is not confirmed.

source.publisher: Department of Trade and Industry - Bureau of Philippine Standards (BPS)
